fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Nevada CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Nevada - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity nevada, how to transfer CNA license to nevada, nevada CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Nevada.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Finish an Nevada-approved CNA training program, usually 75 hours long.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of the Nevada nurse aide exam.

steps.title: Submit Your Application
steps.description: Apply for certification, complete any required background check steps, and pay the required fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Nevada CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $50. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Nevada.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Nevada?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 8 to 16 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nevada while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in NV.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Nevada.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Nevada
hero.dek: Nevada CNA certification requires a 75-hour training program, Nevada State Board of Nursing eligibility, and a passing score on the NNAAP (Prometric) exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Nevada
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Nevada CNA certification.
